rationale: In the Methods section, the paper explicitly states that it used the BDM method and cites Wertenbroch and Skiera (2002) in that procedural justification. The cited work is tied to a concrete operational choice (how WTP is measured), not just broad background. Although grouped with Becker et al. (1964), the citation supports a core measurement component used in the empirical design, so the role is substantive. The citing paper does not refine or challenge that prior work; it uses the established approach in a new setting.
relation_description: The citing study uses the BDM willingness-to-pay elicitation approach, citing Wertenbroch and Skiera (2002) as a marketing reference for this incentive-compatible method. It applies this method to collect WTP bids for movie DVDs as a behavioral benchmark alongside EEG-based prediction.
